codeReview.summary: The feature wires horizon scaling for burst/stagnation thresholds, structural-change window, and momentum window, and adds new threshold fields with defaults. However, for tensions with a horizon, the new burst/stagnation threshold fields are effectively ignored because effective_recency replaces the provided threshold with a fixed 10% horizon-width window. This means the claimed configurability does not hold in the horizon-aware path this feature targets.

issues.description: Configurable burst/stagnation thresholds are not actually configurable when horizon is present: detect_compensating_strategy passes thresholds.burst_threshold_seconds and thresholds.stagnation_threshold_seconds into effective_recency (lines 2051-2054), but effective_recency ignores absolute_recency for Some(horizon) and always returns ~10% of horizon width (lines 833-845). As a result, changing those threshold fields has no effect for horizon-based tensions.

VAL-DFX-004 test coverage is weak for the day-vs-year differential: the day-horizon result is computed into _cs_day but never asserted, while only cs_year.is_none() is checked (line 7754). This can miss regressions where both paths return None for unrelated reasons.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The behavior of effective_recency with horizons (ignoring absolute_recency and returning 10% of horizon width) is not clearly documented in shared state, and it caused significant implementation/test churn in this feature session.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents horizon scaling generally but not this specific override behavior; worker transcript skeleton for session 7445e409-3e9e-4240-9508-2671ce2bf04c explicitly notes: 'effective_recency completely ignores absolute value when a horizon is present'.

summary: Fail: horizon scaling and momentum scaling were implemented, but configurable burst/stagnation thresholds do not take effect for horizon-bearing tensions due to effective_recency semantics; this is a blocking mismatch against the feature’s configurability goal.
